'''Jon Bennison''' (born 1 December 2002) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er and {{rlp|fb}} for the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]]. 

Previously Bennison has played for [[St Helens RFC|St Helens]] and [[Widnes Vikings]].

==Playing career==
===St Helens===
Throughout the 2021 Under-19s season, Bennison made eight appearances, scoring seven tries across the campaign.

On 17 September 2021, Bennison made his professional debut, scoring a try in St Helens' 26–14 loss to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Salford Red Devils v Saints |url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/matches/2021/first-team/salford-red-devils-v-saints-2021-09-17/ |website=saintsrlfc.com |date=18 September 2021 |access-date=19 September 2021}}</ref>
On 24 September 2022, Bennison scored a try during St Helens 2022 Super League Grand Final victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|newspaper=The Observer|date=24 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>
Bennison played 19 games for St Helens in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished third on the table. Bennison played in St Helens narrow loss against the Catalans Dragons in the semi-final which ended St Helens four-year dominance of the competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23856044.paul-wellens-q-saints-review-year-look-2024/|title=Paul Wellens Q&A: Saints review of year and a look to 2024|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk|date=14 October 2023 }}</ref>
Bennison played 15 matches for St Helens in the 2024 Super League season as the club finished sixth on the table. He played in St Helens golden point extra-time playoff loss against Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/sep/28/warrington-st-helens-super-league-eliminator-playoff-rugby-league-match-report/|title=
‘Soul destroying’: Warrington eliminate St Helens in golden drop goal thriller|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
On 14 September 2025, it was announced that Bennison would be departing St Helens at the end of the 2025 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cly616xz15eo/|title=Bennison to leave St Helens at end of the season|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings (loan)===
On 29 July 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=29 Jul 2025|accessdate=31 Jul 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Jon Bennison seals Championship deal as St Helens exit confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jon-bennison-seals-championship-deal-as-st-helens-exit-confirmed}}</ref>

On 10 September 2025 it was announced that he had been recalled from his season long loan ahead of crucial clash with the [[Leigh Leopards]] at the weekend<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|author=Ben Olawumi|date=10 Sep 2025|title=St Helens recall off-contract star from Championship loan ahead of Leigh clash|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-recall-off-contract-star-from-championship-loan-ahead-of-leigh-clash}}</ref>

===York Knights===
On 14 October 2025, Bennison signed for [[York Knights|York]] ahead of the 2026 season.<ref>{{cite news |title=York Knights sign Jon Bennison to strengthen Super League bid |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/york-knights-sign-jon-bennison-to-strengthen-super-league-bid-5359237 |access-date=14 October 2025 |work=Yorkshire Post |date=14 October 2025 |language=en}}</ref>
